shared_types.AuditEventKind: enum(EvaluationCompleted, AnomalyEscalated, AnomalyResolved, PolicySetUpdated, PatternRecorded, SuggestionGenerated, ClassificationPerformed)
shared_types.Predicate: enum(GreaterThan(int), LessThan(int), GreaterThanOrEqual(int), LessThanOrEqual(int), EqualTo(ClaimValue), NotEqualTo(ClaimValue), InSet(list))
shared_types.JsonRpcRequest: struct(jsonrpc: str, method: str, params: any, id: str)
shared_types.JsonRpcResponse: struct(jsonrpc: str, result: any, error: RootError, id: str)

conventions: **Rust** (latest stable, nightly only for bulletproofs crate)
Tests via `cargo test` — unit tests in-module, integration tests in `tests/`
**aws-lc-rs** for all FIPS-path primitives (Ed25519, X25519, AES-256-GCM, HKDF-SHA-256)
No custom crypto — use established libraries for every primitive
Zero secrets in memory after use
Constant-time operations for all secret-dependent code paths
Vault server never has access to plaintext
Tier 3 compartment keys are never held by the agent
Agent sessions expire and are revocable
All proofs are domain-bound and time-limited
One-way data flow: vault -> agent -> service, never reverse
All disclosures are on the audit chain
ANOMALY decisions are never silently resolved
No algorithm negotiation in any protocol
No `unwrap()` or `expect()` in library code — Result everywhere
Errors must not leak sensitive information (no raw key material in error messages)
Crypto failures return generic error types (no oracle)
Unit tests for every public function
Property-based tests for crypto operations (round-trip, known-answer)
The BBS+ unlinkability invariant must be statistically tested
Tier 3 structural isolation must be tested (agent session cannot derive Tier 3 keys)
Policy engine anomaly detection must be tested against role/predicate matrix
`///` on every public type and function
Architecture decisions include threat model rationale
